purpose: The commit modifies hci_cmd_sync_queue to return an error if the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set, preventing the queuing of commands when the device is being unregistered to avoid a Use-After-Free (UAF) vulnerability.
The commit removes a redundant and 'stray' include of <asm/termios.h> from n_hdlc.c, as the required definitions are already provided via <linux/tty.h>.
The commit updates the documentation for the 'perf c2c' tool, specifically adding the 'N/A' metric for store operations to the report table description.

The commit fixes race conditions and memory management issues related to the hugetlb vma lock. It ensures new vma locks are allocated during vma splitting to avoid double-freeing, and modifies the range unmapping process to free the vma lock instead of just clearing VM_MAYSHARE to prevent subsequent pmd sharing.
The commit updates the Device Tree bindings for the Realtek RT5682S codec to formally document the AVDD and MICVDD power supply properties, which were already being used in some device tree files.
The commit fixes a memory resource leak in the zorro7xx_remove_one function by adding a missing iounmap() call to ensure that memory-mapped I/O regions are properly freed when the device is removed.
The commit removes an unused field ('work') from the 'mlx5e_async_ctx' structure in the kTLS implementation of the mlx5e driver.
The commit changes the type of the `key_idx` member in the `cfg80211_auth_request` struct from unsigned to signed to allow the value -1 to be assigned without triggering static analysis warnings.
The commit sorts the CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS configuration option in the RISC-V defconfigs to match the output of savedefconfig, ensuring consistency in the configuration files.
The commit introduces a helper function `mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode()` to encapsulate the check for whether the chip's FIFOs are in CAN-FD mode, replacing existing inline checks.
The commit modifies the RTC selftests to skip execution when a Real Time Clock (RTC) device is not present in the system, preventing unnecessary test failures.
The commit removes a redundant assignment to the 'bytes' variable in sound/pci/echoaudio/midi.c, which was identified as dead code by a clang scan build warning.

The commit decouples the 'rcuscale' tool from the dependency on 'RCU Tasks' in kernels configured with CONFIG_PREEMPT_NONE=y, preventing the unnecessary inclusion of RCU Tasks when only rcuscale is desired.
The commit removes a duplicated phrase 'be split be split' in a comment within the spte.h header file, correcting a typo.

The commit reverts a previous change that populated the dcn315 clock table based on dcfclk. This is done to avoid a 'no read' situation and potential underflow caused by how the power management firmware (pmfw) fills the table when dcfclk states are disabled.
The commit adds a compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Mali-G31 GPU to the Device Tree bindings documentation.
The commit renames the structure `pcie_port` to `dw_pcie_rp` to align with the naming convention of the DesignWare PCIe core driver, ensuring consistency across the codebase.
The commit fixes a compiler warning (-Wformat) in the amd-xgbe driver by changing the format specifier from %hx to %x to match the promoted 'int' type of the argument.
The commit fixes a signed/unsigned comparison warning in the MIPS jump_label mechanism by casting the upper bound of the branch range check to a long to ensure a signed comparison is performed.

The commit removes a specific case (HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD) from a switch statement in SetHwReg8188EU() and calls the associated function directly instead, as part of a cleanup effort to remove the HAL layer.
The commit marks non-global symbols in arm64 kernel code as static to resolve sparse warnings about undeclared symbols.

The commit removes incorrect interrupt flags cells from MDSS nodes in the Device Tree (DT) bindings for msm display,msm. This is because the MDSS interrupt provider is a single-cell provider, and providing flags on the consumer side is technically incorrect for this specific hardware/provider.
The commit modifies struct ctrl_ref to allocate memory for arrays, preparing the codebase for future functionality where drivers can change array sizes.
The commit adds support for several new Qualcomm SoCs (SM8350, SC8280XP, SA8540P, SM8450) and associated PMICs to the socinfo driver, expanding the driver's hardware compatibility list.
The commit expands the range of recognized chip cuts (versions) for rtl8xxxu drivers, extending the support from A-E to A-P (0-15) to avoid printing 'unknown' for higher versions.
The commit removes a redundant software enable count (.is_enabled state) from the PRCMU clocks in the ux500 driver, as the clock framework core already maintains a software enable count.

The commit reorganizes and groups #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL directives in the RISC-V memory management initialization code to reduce redundancy and improve code structure by grouping related declarations and macros.
The commit enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware in the Device Tree Source (DTS) for the sm6350-lena board, allowing future I2C nodes to function correctly.
The commit updates comments in the rzg2l thermal driver to improve clarity, fix a typo ('caliberation' to 'calibration'), and align with Linux kernel coding style regarding comment placement.

The commit fixes an infinite loop in the `release_swfw_sync` function of the igc driver. It prevents the driver from hanging when it cannot acquire the HW semaphore, specifically during hardware surprise-removal scenarios.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to replace outdated ASoC DAI clocking terminology/defines with modern equivalents to align with current kernel standards.
The commit removes a conditional check for IS_SWAPFILE, as the macro is always defined, making the check redundant and pointless.

The commit implements the GTP echo response mechanism in the kernel. It ensures that GTP_ECHO_REQ packets are automatically responded to with GTP_ECHO_RSP, including the necessary recovery tag and restart counter, ensuring compliance with the GTP specification.

The commit replaces calls to cpumask_next() and cpumask_next_zero() with cpumask_first() and a newly created cpumask_first_zero() when the starting index is -1, simplifying the logic for finding the first set/unset bit in a cpumask.

The commit removes a call to the 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR from the brcmfmac driver, as the firmware no longer supports this call, causing it to consistently fail and spam the console with error messages during roaming.
The commit adds a new board configuration (adl_rt5682) for the Intel SOF driver to support a specific hardware platform using the alc5682 codec without a speaker amplifier, connecting it to SSP0.
The commit ensures that zerocopy sendmsg requests in io_uring are failed immediately if the underlying socket does not support zerocopy, mirroring a previous fix for zerocopy send requests.

The commit modifies the documentation preamble to check for the existence of xeCJK.sty before attempting to load it, preventing a LaTeX error when the package is missing on certain distributions like RHEL 9.
The commit modifies the checkpatch.pl script to stop reporting errors for macros that use the 'case' keyword, allowing these specific types of macros to pass the kernel's static analysis tool.
The commit replaces simple-KMS helpers with regular atomic helpers in the simpledrm driver to improve architecture and facilitate code sharing, explicitly stating there are no functional changes.
The commit introduces a jpeg decode worker interface for the mtk-jpegdec driver to enable parallel execution across three hardware units on the MT8195 SoC.
The commit removes redundant VPRINTK() debug print statements and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() function from the libata driver, as the information is now provided by tracepoints and SCSI tracing.

The commit adds support for entering 'zstate' (a power-saving state) during extended vblank periods associated with FreeSync Video mode. It calculates the time gained from extra vblank lines and triggers zstate entry if the time exceeds 5ms, while updating the min_dst_y_next_start register.

The commit fixes a type mismatch between a function pointer prototype (hqd_destroy) and its implementations, which caused a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) failure/crash during boot on specific hardware with CONFIG_CFI_CLANG enabled.
The commit corrects a typo in a Device Tree binding property name from 'reset' to 'resets' and adds 'additionalProperties: false' to the schema to ensure future validation errors are caught.
The commit silences a GCC 12 array-bounds warning in ath9k/mac.c by moving the warning to W=1+ (higher warning levels), effectively suppressing it for default build configurations.
The commit replaces the function call `pm_runtime_get_sync` with `pm_runtime_resume_and_get` in the sun8i-ss crypto driver to simplify the power management runtime API usage.

The commit moves the logic for installing and removing fixed files in io_uring, relocating it to the filetable code to improve organization. It also changes the helper functions' signatures to accept a context (ctx) instead of an io_kiocb, as the operation is ring-level rather than request-level.

The commit updates Device Tree bindings for the NXP i.MX8MQ VPU to split G1 and G2 decoder blocks into separate nodes, reflecting their independent nature and supporting the vpu-blk-ctrl power-domain controller driver.
The commit removes the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro and merges its contents directly into the IWL_DEVICE_BZ macro to simplify the code structure.
